channel.title: Bars on Gourmand Syndrome
channel.description: Recent content in Bars on Gourmand Syndrome

item.title: Penthouse 808
item.description: Summary This bar / restaurant is located on the 6th floor in the Ravel Hotel in Long Island City. The view is fairly nice as you can see Midtown from the east, but it is not stellar since it sits slightly lower than the Queensborough bridge and there are 2 smoke stack obstructions as you look towards the Empire State building. We ordered some bottles of Rose and an entree.

item.title: The Polynesian
item.description: Summary This place is located near 42nd Street and 9 ave in the POD hotel. The restaurant decor is kind of interesting as it does give out a tropical-ish vibe to it. There is an outdoor area that has a lot of those basket woven seats with a cushion on them. From where we sat outdoors, we can see views of the H&M building and the Empire State Building in plain sight.

item.title: The 18th Room
item.description: Cool speak easy spot right next to Bathtub Gin. Seems to be Cuban driven. It has a very jazzy speak easy feel to it. The theme of the menu is pretty cool. On Wednesdays, they have a live cuban band. The bartenders can craft drinks based on what you’re looking for. I think weekdays are probably the best to come here. The music on the speakers is good and isn’t loud, and you can actually hear people.

item.title: The Huntress Whiskey N Wings
item.description: Summary The vibe in this place was pretty cool. It reminded me of being in one of those Lord of the Rings tavern. The poutine was pretty salty, but it was a good match with the beers. I wish the cheese curds were a little warmer and more abundant. The savage is actually regular hot wings. The hot wings tasted like they were fried first then doused in sauce after.

item.title: Spritzenhaus 33
item.description: Crowded AF. Really noisy, bartenders slow AF. Standard Biergarten is better as well as the other one in Billsburg.

item.title: Hotel Delmano
item.description: Cool place. They have food. Not too expensive. Right next to the water sorta in Williamsburg.

item.title: Due West
item.description: Great bar/vibe. This new-ish place is definitely going to be popular.

item.title: The Ragtrader and Bo Beep Cocktail
item.description: Interesting bar. Bar upstairs is cool. Piano bar downstairs is really KEWL.

item.title: The Otheroom
item.description: Good date vibe in the West Village.

item.title: Elsa
item.description: Cool vibe. Good place to take people who live in Carroll Gardens area circa Smith St.

item.title: Dutch Kills
item.description: Decent vibey bar similar to some LES places, but way out in Queens. But who knows maybe Long Island City might be the next spot after Williamsburg!

item.title: Public Hotel
item.description: People come here to drink because they’re retarded. Terrible drinks. College-ish atmosphere. Pretentious for no reason. Oh, it has the orange escalator hallway that everyone likes to take pictures of. There’s also that purple glass window back that people like too.

item.title: Dear Irving
item.description: Good old boys bar. Think jazzy-ish. Nice place.

item.title: Fresh Kills
item.description: Decent stop for a drink before going elsewhere.

item.title: Post Office
item.description: Cool place for whiskeys. But it’s CLOSED.

item.title: Attaboy
item.description: You tell them what ingredients you like in your drink, and they make something magical for you.

item.title: Golden Years
item.description: Divey cash-only bar that is decent. Across the street from Fette Sau.

item.title: Troost
item.description: Cool divey spot. Cheap drinks. Lots of gin/tequila based cocktails. They occasionally have random small bands.

item.title: The Spotted Owl Tavern
item.description: Ok spot.

item.title: Mother of Pearl
item.description: Interesting decor. They have a shark drink where it has a bunch of blood on it. Notice a bunch of people bring their dates here.

item.title: Mayahuel
item.description: Was a Mezcal speak easy place when I went. Decent mezcal based cocktails. It had a change of ownership recently.

item.title: Copper and Oak
item.description: This place is cool. All scotches and nice ambiance.

item.title: Standard Biergarten
item.description: Vibe reminds me of Wurstkuche in Los Angeles. But without the distinct dogs and beers. Still cool though.

item.title: Employees Only
item.description: It’s alright. Bar gets crowded. Food is decent. It’s a has been.

item.title: Raines Law Room
item.description: Cool place. It’s in some random building basement, and the couches are comfy. Feels a little noir-ish.
